message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed lever for improving a RACE score. Default to refuted=true when uncertain.

LEVER: Synthesis layer: an 'argue' pass exempt from span-grounding
TARGETS: insight (0.32) / Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation w=0.25; Emergent Themes / Theoretical Linkages w=0.20; Analytical Depth in Mechanisms w=0.25 (weight 0.32)
CHANGE: Add a compose-stage pass AFTER facts are assembled per section: for each section, generate 2-3 interpretive paragraphs that (a) reconcile conflicting numbers already in the section (e.g. our body already contains Humlum's precise-null Danish results [95] next to 15% RCT gains [42] and never says the obvious thing the reference says at its line 100 about lags and scale), (b) state which pattern the evidence supports and where it disagrees, (c) tie back to the displacement/reinstatement lens. Verification rule for these paragraphs: NOT span-grounding but entailment — every interpretive claim must be entailed by ≥2 already-verified facts it cites. Also generate one thesis paragraph in the intro (the reference's 'promise and peril' duality move) and rewrite the conclusion as consensus → disagreements → named research gaps, each bullet derived from tensions the argue pass surfaced.
CLAIMED GAIN: +0.020 to +0.030 (insight 0.424 → ~0.49; the reference's ~13 citation-free interpretive paragraphs are the single largest measured difference between the texts)
CLAIMED FAILURE MODE: Three ways: (1) the model writes generic hedged filler ('the evidence is mixed') that the judge reads as padding — enforce that each paragraph must name ≥2 specific cited findings and take a position on their relationship; (2) entailment-checking is weaker than span-verify, so an over-claim slips through and costs credibility; (3) at fixed 9k words the synthesis displaces facts and comprehensiveness dips — pay for it out of the padding cut (next lever), not out of coverage.

ATTACK IT THROUGH THIS LENS:
NOISE: is the claimed gain distinguishable from the measured +/-0.016 noise floor on a single task? If not, it is unfalsifiable as specified.

Context: our report /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md scored 0.4313. Reference /home/polaris/polaris_project/RACE_REFERENCE_task72.md = 0.5000 by definition. SOTA = 0.5265.
Read the files if you need to check a claim. Be adversarial, concrete, and brief.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# POLARIS OVERNIGHT PROGRESS (on-box driver, Opus 4.8)

Driver session started 2026-07-11 13:54 UTC. Operator asleep; trail lives here + in git.

## READ THIS FIRST AT WAKE-UP — two blockers changed the plan

**BLOCKER 1 — the three wheel worktrees are NOT writable by the box driver.**
`/workspace/compose_wt`, `/workspace/outline_agent_wt`, `/workspace/outline_tooluse_wt` are all
`root:root 755`. The box driver runs as uid 1000 (`polaris`) and has **no sudo**. Every wheel the
brief assigns me is therefore read-only to me, as are the root-owned processes running inside them.

Workaround (deviation from the brief, deliberate, and the operator should know):
I created polaris-owned worktrees from the **exact same commits**, one per wheel, so the
one-worktree-per-wheel rule still holds and the trail still lands in git:

| wheel | brief's worktree (root, read-only to me) | my worktree (writable) | branch | forked from |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| agentic-outline | /workspace/outline_agent_wt | /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent | bot/outline-agent-box | ecda022 |
| tool-use compute | /workspace/outline_tooluse_wt | /home/polaris/wt/tooluse | bot/outline-tooluse-box | ecda022 |
| compose depth | /workspace/compose_wt | /home/polaris/wt/compose | bot/compose-box | eff82fb |

Merging these back is a fast-forward-able branch merge; no work is stranded.

**BLOCKER 2 — "serve the compose model locally on the A100" is not physically possible on this box.**
The brief's compose unlock assumes we can serve the compose model locally to escape the OpenRouter
429 ceiling. The compose model is `z-ai/glm-5.2` (confirmed in the live logs). That is a ~355B-class
MoE. This box has:
- **114 GB free disk** — the FP8 weights alone are ~355 GB. They do not fit on disk, let alone VRAM.
- **80 GB free VRAM** (GPU0). GPU1 has 31.7 GB held by an **orphaned root-owned vLLM EngineCore**
  (PID 8846, PPID 1, no listening port, running since Jul 08). It is dead weight and I **cannot kill
  it** — it is root-owned and I have no sudo. Operator: `sudo kill -9 8846` reclaims 31.7 GB.
- vLLM is **not installed** in the box python (torch 2.5.1 + transformers 5.8.1 are).

So "serve GLM-5.2 locally" is off the table regardless of effort. What IS feasible locally is the
*verifier* side (a small NLI model), which is the high-call-volume, low-complexity half of the
workload. That is the honest version of the unlock and it is what I am pricing out.

**BLOCKER 3 — I cannot stop the running compose jobs.** Two root-owned `run_s5_i3.py` runs
(PIDs 958596, 966951) launched by the laptop at 12:55 and 13:08 are still hammering OpenRouter with
glm-5.2 and are **self-contending for the same rate limit** (15x HTTP 429 already in one log). I can
neither kill nor deprioritize them. They will keep distorting any compose timing I measure.

## Time budget
Driver window is ~13:54 -> ~15:00 UTC = **~65 minutes**, not a full night. Scope is triaged
accordingly: I will not pretend to land a 34-tool toolkit + 22-case battery + local serving stack in
an hour. Priority is (1) truth on the record, (2) the single highest-value wheel actually landed and
independently gated.

## HEADLINE — the two wheels were each bricked at ONE LINE, not missing features

The single most important thing the operator should read this morning:

**1. The compose ceiling was NEVER the OpenRouter 429. It was a self-inflicted serialization.**
`_draft_passes_wrapper` (`generator/abstractive_writer.py:661`) is a plain sync `def`. It was called
with **no `await`** from inside the async coroutine `_pre_pass_one_basket` (`:749`), at two call
sites. It loops sentences -> `writer_verify_fn` -> `provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance`
-> `entailment_judge.judge()` -> `_post_with_total_deadline` -> `fut.result(timeout=total_s)` — which
**blocks the calling thread**. The calling thread is the asyncio event loop.

So **every NLI judge POST froze the entire compose event loop.** Achieved verify concurrency was
**1**, regardless of `PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12` or `PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16`. The
"~48-way parallel" compose was 48-way *only between* verifications. Every writer call in every
section stalled behind each single NLI POST.

This reframes the whole compose thesis in the brief. We were tuning 429 backoff and pricing out a
$355GB local serving stack to escape a rate limit that **was not the binding constraint.**

FIXED (`bot/compose-box` @ `0615bc5`): both call sites now `await asyncio.to_thread(...)`.
**Fable gate: SIGN_OFF, 0 P0/P1 — with an empirical A/B probe**, not just a code read:

| | elapsed (4 baskets) | verify thread | event loop |
|---|---|---|---|
| HEAD | 2.06s (serialized) | `MainThread` | frozen; heartbeat starved throughout |
| fixed | 0.60s (parallel) | `asyncio_0..3` | live; max heartbeat gap 0.050s |

The judge machinery was **already built for this** and had been waiting: `judge_verdict_cache` is
`RLock`-guarded and its own docstring says it is "shared across the ThreadPool verifier workers";
the judge holds per-thread httpx clients via `threading.local`; POSTs are bounded by a
`BoundedSemaphore`. All of it was moot because of one missing `await`.

**2. The compute stack is not dormant — it is advertised and guaranteed to fail.**
`outline_agent.py:1579` hardcoded `client=None` into every tool dispatch. `execute_python` is
registered, is `requires_llm=True`, and IS listed to the decide LLM as available — and returns
`success=False, "No LLM client available"` (`tool_registry.py:556`) on **100% of calls**. It was not
in `_tool_failure_gap_check`'s watched set either, so the failure was **silent**: the agent moved on
and the number got written from evidence prose instead of computed.

PARTIALLY fixed (`bot/outline-tooluse-box` @ `1b2e3c7`) — **this wheel is NOT signed off**, see below.

## NEXT ACTIONS, in priority order (start here)

1. **[compose] Tune `PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY` against a measured run.** The `to_thread` fix
   makes this knob *live on the compose path* for the first time — it is now the binding constraint
   on the verify phase (default **4**). Raising it is the actual throughput unlock for "render all
   346 baskets fast". I did **NOT** raise it blind: the default is deliberate storm protection, the
   judge shares the GLM-5.2 model *and the OpenRouter account* with the writer, and I could not get a
   clean measurement while the root-owned jobs hammered the same rate limit. Needs one clean A/B.
   (Correction to my own earlier claim: this knob was NOT a global no-op — it was already binding on
   the credibility/strict-verify `to_thread` paths. It was non-binding *only* on the compose path.)
2. **[tooluse] The computed value is still UNREACHABLE.** This is the open Fable P1 and the reason

FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:34:- 2026-07-12 | Rank5 LANDED (b1f3f68) | Deletion now requires affirmative OFF_SUBJECT in **BOTH independent shuffled passes, at EVERY tier** (the old rail escalated only T1/T2, leaving the same boundary noise unchecked everywhere else); any disagreement KEEPS. T1/T2 escalation retained **on top** as a third look (can only rescue). **SAME-WORK GUARD** added: never delete a row whose same-work sibling survived ON_TOPIC — and note the bug I caught by checking the real data shape: `same_work_groups` arrive as **dicts** with `member_evidence_ids`, not bare id lists, so my first cut would have **silently matched nothing**. A safety rail that quietly no-ops is worse than no rail. The fraction ceiling is **reclassified from policy knob to OUTAGE TRIPWIRE (0.75)** — it now catches only the failure a per-row rule cannot (a systemic fault where BOTH passes are garbage and agree), and can still only ever REFUSE, never widen a delete set. **NET (offline dry-run vs recorded verdicts): 518 both-pass − 5 same-work = 513 deletable, 484 kept, ZERO seminal papers deleted** (Acemoglu/Autor/Brynjolfsson, "GPTs are GPTs", "Generative AI at Work" all survive).
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:63:- 2026-07-12 21:50 | **FABLE GATE: LOCK A *REFUTED* — MY CONFOUND THESIS WAS WRONG, AND THE REAL CEILING IS NOW NAMED.** I argued the ~51%-alien menu was what strict_verify was killing (=> kept_fraction 0.35, => clean pool would grow the report for free). **strict_verify is TOPICALITY-BLIND.** `strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool)` (provenance_generator.py:3598) never takes the research question, and its NLI judge is `judge(self, sentence, span)` (entailment_judge.py:740) prompted *"Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE"* (:584) — no question, no topic, no section title. **A sentence faithfully written from a real-but-ALIEN source is entailed by its span and is KEPT.** Off-topic evidence therefore produces kept-but-IRRELEVANT sentences, never dropped ones — so it cannot be what kept_fraction measures. (Verified all 3 claims myself against source.) The clean-pool A/B still running is now a FALSIFICATION TEST of Fable's prediction: armed kept_fraction should move <=±0.05. Its real payoff was never length — it is the RELEVANCE of shipped prose, i.e. a RACE-score win.
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:75:- 2026-07-12 22:15 | **FABLE'S PREDICTION IS *NOT* REFUTED — AND I ALMOST CLAIMED IT WAS.** Fable predicted |Δkept_fraction| <= 0.05 (strict_verify is topicality-blind). Observed 0.519 -> 0.605 = **+0.085**, and my comparator printed "PREDICTION BROKEN". **That claim does not survive arithmetic:** kept_fraction is a proportion over ~160 sentence trials; SE(diff) = sqrt(2 * 0.55*0.45/160) ≈ **0.055**, so +0.085 is **~1.5σ — indistinguishable from noise at n=1 per arm.** A single-run aggregate cannot overturn a source-verified structural fact (`judge(sentence, span)` never receives the question). **Recording this as UNRESOLVED, not as a win.** (Same discipline as Rank6: an aggregate is not a mechanism.)
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:112:- 2026-07-13 00:45 | **UNPLANNED GIFT: Rank10 is now a REPLICATION of Rank9** (theme floor = no-op => identical effective config). Every arm tonight has been **n=1**, and I have twice had to reason about noise (the +0.085 kept_fraction, the word-count spread) **without any variance estimate at all**. This run supplies one for free. **I will read Rank10 as a variance probe, not as a new arm** — and any Rank9-vs-Rank10 gap is the noise floor against which every claim tonight must be re-read.
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:114:- 2026-07-13 01:35 | **THE VARIANCE PROBE (Rank10 = Rank9 replicate) — AND IT FORCES ME TO WITHDRAW A CLAIM AND REINSTATE ONE I HAD KILLED.** Same effective config, two runs: verified **217 vs 219 (±1%)**, kept_fraction **0.562 vs 0.572 (±0.01)** — but report words **8055 vs 9300 (±15%)**, body words **6359 vs 7742 (±22%)**, density **13.84 vs 12.14 (±12%)**. **VERIFIED-SENTENCE COUNT AND KEPT_FRACTION ARE STABLE; WORD COUNT AND DENSITY ARE NOT.** Consequences, both directions:
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:115:  - **WITHDRAWN: "the cap raised density 12.52 -> 13.84."** That is **+1.32 against a measured noise floor of 1.70** — inside the noise. I cannot distinguish it from run-to-run variance and I am not going to pretend otherwise. (The stacked cap's kept_fraction gain, 0.512 -> 0.562/0.572 against ±0.01 noise, **does** survive — the cap is real, my *reason* for liking it was not.)
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:116:  - **REINSTATED — AND IT WAS *MY* CAUTION THAT WAS WRONG:** I logged the CTRL->ARMED kept_fraction move (+0.085) as "~1.5σ, unresolved noise" using a **binomial SE of 0.055**. The empirical replicate says the true noise on kept_fraction is **~±0.01**, so +0.085 is **~8x the noise: Fable's prediction (|Δ|<=0.05) IS REFUTED.** My binomial model assumed sentence-level independence and **overestimated** the variance (Fable argued clustering would make it *bigger*; the measurement says smaller). **Both of us reasoned about noise for hours without ever measuring it. One replicate settled it in 50 minutes.** Caveat kept on the record: n=2 is a weak variance estimate, and the two runs share a judge seed and corpus, so they are not fully independent draws.
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:117:  - **STANDING RULE FROM NOW ON: report VERIFIED SENTENCES and DISTINCT WORKS (stable). Treat any word-count or density gap under ~20% as NOISE.**
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:118:- 2026-07-13 01:35 | **WHAT SURVIVES THE NOISE FLOOR — THE REAL SCORECARD.** Baseline -> best (Rank10): **words 3408 -> 9300 (2.7x)**, **verified sentences 81 -> 219 (2.7x, on the STABLE metric)**, **distinct works cited 42 -> 94 (2.2x)**, kept_fraction 0.519 -> 0.572, sections 10 -> 10. Density fell 16.88 -> 12.14 (-28%, beyond noise — **real, and the honest cost of the depth push**). **Frontier floor is 10k words; we are at 9.3k.** Levers that got us here, both of which were **already in the code and switched off**: `PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET` (Rank7, built tonight at the one prompt seam) + `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (pre-existing, never once enabled).
FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:121:- 2026-07-13 02:15 | **THE FINDING THAT MATTERS MOST TONIGHT — AND IT IS THE OPERATOR'S IDEA, WHICH I KILLED AT 21:55: TIER COMPLIANCE COLLAPSED AS THE REPORT GREW.** Cited-entry T1-T3 share: **CTRL 57.8% -> RANK7 53.7% -> RANK9 45.4% -> RANK10 43.3%.** DRB task 72 instructs *"only cite high-quality, English-language journal articles"* — so **the depth push made INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING WORSE**, and this metric is **deterministic (no noise)**. Rank10 cites a **Toptal freelancer listicle, an amplifai blog, apporya.com, a Substack, hrexecutive.com**, while **112 T1-T3 works that survived the judge are never cited at all.**

    flywheel: the variance probe -- withdraw one claim, reinstate one I had killed

    runs: verified 217 vs 219 (+/-1%), kept_fraction 0.562 vs 0.572 (+/-0.01), but
    report words 8055 vs 9300 (+/-15%), body words 6359 vs 7742 (+/-22%), density
    13.84 vs 12.14 (+/-12%). Verified-sentence count and kept_fraction are STABLE;
    word count and density are NOT.
    
    WITHDRAWN: 'the cap raised density 12.52 -> 13.84'. That is +1.32 against a
    measured noise floor of 1.70 -- inside the noise. The cap's kept_fraction gain
    (0.512 -> 0.562/0.572 vs +/-0.01 noise) does survive; my REASON for liking it did
    not.
    
    REINSTATED, and my caution was the error: I logged CTRL->ARMED kept_fraction
    (+0.085) as '~1.5 sigma, unresolved' on a binomial SE of 0.055. The empirical
    replicate puts the true noise at ~+/-0.01, so +0.085 is ~8x noise and Fable's
    |delta|<=0.05 prediction IS refuted. My binomial model assumed sentence-level
    independence and OVERestimated variance -- Fable argued clustering would make it
    bigger; the measurement says smaller. Both of us argued about noise for hours
    without measuring it. One replicate settled it in 50 minutes. (n=2 is weak, and
    the runs share a judge seed -- noted.)
    
    STANDING RULE: report verified sentences and distinct works (stable). Treat any
    word-count or density gap under ~20% as noise.
    
    SCORECARD, baseline -> best: words 3408 -> 9300 (2.7x), verified sentences 81 ->

# BRIEF: get POLARIS to SOTA on DeepResearch Bench (RACE), task 72

## What RACE actually is (read this first — most of our past reasoning got it wrong)
RACE is REFERENCE-BASED. An LLM judge scores BOTH our report and a human REFERENCE report
against per-task weighted criteria, then:  **Overall = target / (target + reference)**.
- **0.500 = TIED with the human reference.**  Below 0.5 = worse than the reference.
- Judge noise measured on this box: **±0.016** (two identical-config runs scored 0.4155 and 0.4313).
- EVALUATOR: the benchmark switched judges in 2026 (Gemini-2.5-Pro deprecated -> **GPT-5.5**). Our sweep
  used `openai/gpt-5.5`, i.e. the CURRENT evaluator. Our numbers are on the right scale.

## THE TARGET: 0.5265 (SOTA). WE ARE AT 0.4313. THE GAP IS 9.5 POINTS.
| System | Overall |
|---|---|
| **ADORE (rank 1, SOTA)** | **0.5265** |
| Tavily Research | 0.5244 |
| (human reference = parity) | 0.5000 |
| Gemini-2.5-Pro DR (the ORIGINAL paper's number — now STALE) | 0.4888 |
| **POLARIS Rank10 (us, best)** | **0.4313** |
| POLARIS baseline | 0.4052 |
**The top systems have BEATEN the human reference (>0.50).** Beating the reference is NOT enough for SOTA
any more — we must beat it by ~2.7 points. Do NOT plan to 0.4888; that target is a year out of date.
CAVEAT the plan must respect: leaderboard scores are averaged over ALL 100 benchmark tasks; ours is ONE
task (#72). A plan that overfits to task 72 wins nothing. Prefer levers that generalise across tasks.

## The scoreboard (measured tonight, official harness, task 72)
| Arm | Words | Overall | Comprehens. | Insight | Instr-Follow | Readability |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CTRL baseline | 3,343 | 0.4052 | 0.4310 | 0.3839 | 0.4032 | 0.4008 |
| Rank7 (long sections) | 7,615 | 0.4041 | 0.4326 | 0.4070 | 0.3996 | 0.3379 |
| Rank8 (menu cap only) | 4,226 | 0.3957 | 0.4197 | 0.3903 | 0.3907 | 0.3634 |
| Rank9 (both levers) | 7,937 | 0.4155 | 0.4372 | 0.4122 | 0.4241 | 0.3588 |
| **Rank10 (best)** | **9,194** | **0.4313** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | 0.3774 |
Rank9 and Rank10 are the SAME effective config (the theme floor was a no-op) => their 0.0158 gap IS the noise floor.

## DIMENSION WEIGHTS FOR TASK 72 (this is where points live)
**Insight 0.32 | Comprehensiveness 0.29 | Instruction-Following 0.25 | Readability 0.14**
Insight is the single biggest lever. Readability is the only dimension we made WORSE, but it is the
LIGHTEST weight — do not over-invest there.

## THE LENGTH QUESTION IS CLOSED
The human REFERENCE report is **9,029 words**. Rank10 is **9,194 words**. We are already AT reference
length. More words cannot help. Anything that trades insight-per-word for length is now strictly harmful.

## THE TASK PROMPT (verbatim — every instruction here is graded)
"Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the
labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing
significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality,
English-language journal articles."

## KNOWN, UNFIXED DEFECTS (found by Fable's prose gate, all still shipping)
1. **4IR framing is SHALLOW, not absent.** (CORRECTED — verify claims like this yourself, do not trust this brief.)
   The earlier "zero mentions" finding was about RANK7 and is now STALE. Measured in the RANK10 BODY:
   "Fourth Industrial Revolution" x4 in 7,742 body words, "4IR" x0. (A naive whole-file grep says x8 — the
   other 4 are in the BIBLIOGRAPHY. This body-vs-references trap already fooled our own density instrument
   once tonight. Split the body from the `## References` section before counting ANYTHING.)
   All 4 body mentions are NAME-DROPS — 4IR appears as scenery, never as an organising frame. The criteria
   (instruction-following w=0.15 "Integration of AI as a Key Driver of the 4IR"; comprehensiveness w=0.10
   "Grounding in the 4IR Context") demand the review DISCUSS AI *within* that context. So the fix is
   INTEGRATION, not insertion. Compare against how the REFERENCE report frames 4IR — it opens with it.
2. **We cite non-journal sources.** Instruction-following criterion w=0.15 demands "all cited sources are

a lucky RACE-judge draw / judge variance? Decisive for direction:
    * if step3 re-scores ~0.40 => 0.4447 was noise; our floor+depth fixes ARE at champion's true level.
    * if step3 re-scores ~0.4447 => real qualitative (prose/framing) gap beyond structure to find.

## 07-12 ~10:25 UTC — DECISIVE: champion re-scores 0.4291 (RACE noise ~0.016); OUR gap is REAL + qualitative
- step3 champion RE-SCORE = 0.4291 (orig 0.4447) => RACE judge variance ~0.016; champion true level ~0.43.

## 07-12 ~11:10 UTC — QUALITY WHEEL R4: lean/no-residual = 0.4058 (BEST of ours, plateau broke)
- r2_lean_noroute RACE=0.4058 (Comp .421 Ins .375 InstrF .431 Read .400), 2910w/11sec, faithful, bib=22 all-cited no-padding.
- CLIMBING: partA 0.3959 -> lever2 0.3982 -> lean 0.4058. Space NOT exhausted; cleanliness is a real lever.
- KEY: leanest report (2442w body) scored HIGHEST — MORE WORDS != better; quality/cleanliness > volume.
  Lean nearly closed InstrF (.431 vs champ .436) + lifted Readability (.385->.400).
- Remaining gap ~0.023 to champion(0.4291), now concentrated in INSIGHT (.375 vs .414, -0.039 worst dim).
  Insight = analysis/synthesis depth — hardest lever (prior insight-directive A/B failed). Next target.
- Best banked artifact still champion step3 (0.4447/0.4291); best REPRODUCIBLE-fix artifact = lean 0.4058.

- r2_best_combo RACE=0.4245 (Comp .431 Ins .415 InstrF .444 Read .402), 3731w/3249w-body/97-verified/25-refs, faithful.
- vs champion re-score 0.4291: gap only 0.0046, WITHIN RACE judge noise (~0.016) => STATISTICAL PARITY.
- InstrF 0.444 EXCEEDS champion 0.436; Insight 0.415 MATCHES champion 0.414. Combo = floor+top24-depth+lean/no-residual.
- CLIMB: 0.3959 -> 0.3982 -> 0.4058 -> 0.4245. General+faithful+RELIABLE (guaranteed coverage, not a lucky draw).
- HONEST BOUNDS: did NOT beat champion's LUCKY 0.4447 (that re-scores 0.4291); reached champion's TRUE ~0.43
Tier mix: T1 5.5% T2 8.4% T3 18.7% T4 20.8% | T5 2.5% T6(blog) 21% T7 8.6% UNKNOWN 14.5% (~44% low-tier).

  -> strict_verify keeps 97 sentences (34 dropped)
  -> 25 sources cited in the report (~3% of works).
Depth-vs-RACE is an INVERTED-U: 22src=0.406, 25=0.4245, 37(champion)=0.4447 PEAK, 40=0.4225,
54=0.398, 64=0.396. Champion's 37 SYNTHESIZED sources beat both lean(25) and bloat(54-64).

## 2. WHY THE OUTLINE SELECTS SO FEW (ranked mechanisms, file:line)
1. SOFT/primary — the planner SEES the full corpus but the prompt says "NEVER pad a section...to reach a
   count" with NO floor (>=8 only in a code comment; FACET floors at 2). glm-5.2 picks ~4-10/section.

content.text: I have what I need. Three independent same-config/same-text replicate pairs and a prior insight-lever A/B settle this.

input.lever: Synthesis layer: an 'argue' pass exempt from span-grounding (interpretive/entailment-verified paragraphs per section + thesis intro + consensus/disagreement/gaps conclusion)
input.reasoning:
REFUTED AS SPECIFIED — the claimed delta is 1.0–1.5σ of this box's own noise floor, and the acceptance test is the n=1 design the project has already been burned by twice.

1) THE NOISE FLOOR IS BIGGER THAN THE PROJECT HAS BEEN TREATING IT. Three independent same-config / same-text score pairs exist in the record: judge re-score of an IDENTICAL text 0.4447→0.4291 (Δ0.0156, AGENTIC_OUTLINE_FINDINGS.md:21); same-config re-run 0.4317→0.4447 (Δ0.0130, OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md:184); Rank9 vs Rank10 replicate 0.4155→0.4313 (Δ0.0158, SOTA_REVIEW_BRIEF.md:33). Mean |Δ| ≈ 0.0148. These are single draws of |D|, and for a normal E|D| = 0.798·σ_D → σ_D ≈ 0.019, σ_single ≈ 0.013. Note the first of those is PURE JUDGE noise on the SAME BYTES — so pipeline variance (word count varies ±15% run to run, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:114) COMPOUNDS on top of it. σ_D ≈ 0.019 is a floor, not a ceiling.

2) THE CLAIMED GAIN LANDS INSIDE IT. +0.020 = 1.05σ (two-sided p≈0.29). +0.030 = 1.58σ (p≈0.11). Neither is significant at n=1 per arm. Power calc: to resolve δ=0.021 at 80%/α=.05 you need n = 2σ²(1.96+0.84)²/δ² ≈ 7 renders PER ARM (14 full compose runs); δ=0.030 still needs ~4/arm. The flywheel produced 5 scored arms in an entire night, all n=1 (FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:112). So as written, the lever cannot be confirmed OR refuted by the harness that will measure it. Unfalsifiable.

3) YOU CANNOT ESCAPE BY "JUST READING THE INSIGHT DIM." The Rank9→Rank10 replicate moved ALL FOUR dimensions in the SAME direction (Comp +0.0177, Insight +0.0116, InstrF +0.0168, Read +0.0186). Per-dim judge scores are common-mode, not independent draws — which is what you'd expect from one judge call and the T/(T+R) ratio form. So a lucky draw lifts insight along with everything else, and "insight rose while others held" is not a signal the instrument can produce.

4) THE CLAIMED MAGNITUDE EXCEEDS EVERYTHING EVER MEASURED ON THIS DIMENSION. Insight went CTRL 0.3839 → Rank10 0.4238 = +0.040 TOTAL across the entire program — and ~0.012–0.016 of that spread is noise. That +0.040 bought a 2.7x report (3,343→9,194 words, 81→219 verified sentences). This lever claims +0.066 on insight — LARGER than the whole depth push delivered — from ~20-30 paragraphs, with zero pilot data. The supporting evidence ("the reference's ~13 citation-free interpretive paragraphs are the single largest measured difference between the texts") is a text-diff against ONE reference, i.e. correlational on n=1. RACE scores against criteria, not against similarity to the reference.

5) THERE IS ALREADY A DIRECT NEGATIVE FOR THIS LEVER CLASS. OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md:182 — "A/B RESULT (STEP 3 insight directive) — honest negative": directive ON scored 0.4094 vs 0.4447 OFF. The project's own recorded LESSON from it (line 189): "RACE length-bias + run-noise are real; must control length + average runs to trust a lever." This lever proposes exactly a prompt-side insight directive, at fixed length, measured with one run.

6) THE LEVER'S OWN FAILURE MODE (3) HAS PRECEDENT. Rank7 raised insight +0.023 over CTRL and OVERALL WENT DOWN (0.4041 vs 0.4052) because Readability collapsed 0.4008→0.3379. At fixed 9k words, insight-targeted prose that displaces facts hits Comprehensiveness (w=0.29) and Instruction-Following (w=0.25) — together 0.54 of weight vs insight's 0.32. A +0.066 insight gain is fully cancelled by a −0.039 average drop on those two.
input.salvage: The MECHANISM may still be real — what is dead is the acceptance test, and this lever uniquely admits a cheap fix the others do not. Because the argue pass is ADDITIVE and POST-COMPOSE, you can build both arms from ONE render: arm A = report as-is; arm B = byte-identical report + the argue paragraphs. That is a PAIRED comparison on the same pipeline draw, which removes the entire pipeline-variance component (the ±15% word-count wobble) and leaves only judge noise, σ≈0.013. Then score each arm k=5 times with the judge and compare means: SE(diff) = 0.013·sqrt(2/5) ≈ 0.008, which resolves a +0.021 delta at ~2.6σ for the cost of 10 judge calls and ZERO extra compose runs. Pre-register the primary endpoint as OVERALL (not insight — see point 3), pre-register the direction, and pre-register a comprehensiveness/instruction-following non-inferiority bound (drop >0.015 on either = kill), because at fixed length those two carry 0.54 of the weight. Do NOT run this as another n=1 arm; if it is run that way the result is uninterpretable whichever way it lands, and the project will spend a night arguing about a 1σ number again — exactly as it did with the +0.085 kept_fraction and the +1.32 density.
